title: Griddle Cooked Polish Macaroni Pasta
description: A wine-infused, chewy noodles sautéed on a griddle with fresh vegetables and herbs, finished with a drizzle of good olive oil for a sattvic-friendly, weeknight Polish comfort dish.
instructions: Preheat the tava-griddle over medium heat and lightly oil the surface.
Add the sliced onion to the hot griddle and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the garlic, bell pepper, carrots, and mushrooms, cooking for another 5 minutes until vegetables soften.
Push the vegetables to one side of the griddle and pour the wine into the cleared space, letting it simmer and reduce until it thickens slightly.
Add the macaroni to the griddle, stirring to coat the noodles in the wine reduction and vegetables.
Sprinkle the dried oregano, salt, and pepper over the pasta, then pour the vegetable broth, letting the mixture simmer gently for 12 minutes.
Dedicate the last 4 minutes to stir in the chopped parsley, allowing the flavors to meld.
Once the macaroni is tender but still chewy, drizzle the extra virgin olive oil over the top and give one final gentle toss.
Remove the griddle from heat, transfer the dish to a serving platter, and garnish with a light sprinkle of parsley.
Serve hot, accompanied by a glass of chilled white wine if desired.
